If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-09T09:55:10.8776410Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T09:50:36.828993683Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57994,\"title\":\"[SQL] Docs: Update README for 1.4.0 release — key concepts, examples, and deprecated API guidance\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57982 by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe 1.4.0 GA release was published on 2026-04-09. Changes include:\\n- Upgraded API version to **2025-01-01**\\n- All classes and methods dependent on the retiring **2014-04-01** API version were marked `[Obsolete]` (from beta.2)\\n- AOT compatibility was enabled (from beta.2)\\n- Upgraded dependent `Azure.Core` to 1.52.0 and `Azure.ResourceManager` to 1.14.0\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No SQL-specific Key Concepts content** — the `## Key concepts` section only has a generic link to Azure SDK guidelines with no explanation of SQL resource types, hierarchy (`SqlServerResource` → `SqlDatabaseResource`), or management patterns\\n- **No SQL-specific Examples with code snippets** — the `## Examples` section only links to a generic samples page; the README has no inline code examples showing common operations (create server, create database, list elastic pools, etc.)\\n- **No migration guidance for deprecated 2014-04-01 API methods** — the 1.4.0 release marks many methods as obsolete (retiring 2014-04-01 API), but the README and Troubleshooting section offer no guidance for users who need to migrate away from them\\n- **README missing the new API version capabilities** — the README does not mention the newly supported `2025-01-01` API version or what features it brings\\n- **`## Documentation` section is non-standard** — the README includes a separate `## Documentation` section that does not appear in the canonical template (see `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md`); its content should be folded into `## Key concepts`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the current minimal `## Key concepts` section with substantive content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` library provides access to the Azure SQL resource hierarchy. The main entry points are:\\n\\n- **`SqlServerResource`** — represents an Azure SQL logical server. Retrieved via `resourceGroup.GetSqlServers()` or `resourceGroup.GetSqlServer(serverName)`.\\n- **`SqlDatabaseResource`** — represents a database inside a logical server. Retrieved via `server.GetSqlDatabases()` or `server.GetSqlDatabase(databaseName)`.\\n- **`SqlElasticPoolResource`** — represents an elastic pool for sharing compute resources across multiple databases.\\n- **`ManagedInstanceResource`** — represents an Azure SQL Managed Instance.\\n- **`ManagedDatabaseResource`** — represents a database inside a Managed Instance.\\n\\nAll long-running operations (Create, Update, Delete) return an `ArmOperation` or `ArmOperation` and accept a `WaitUntil` parameter to control whether the call blocks until completion.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Methods that target the retired 2014-04-01 REST API version are marked `[Obsolete]` as of version 1.4.0. Refer to the [SQL REST API retirement notice]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for details and migration guidance.\\n```\\n\\nRemove the separate `## Documentation` section and incorporate its quickstart/API-reference links into the `## Key concepts` section or `## Next steps`.\\n\\nReplace the current `## Examples` section content with a snippet placeholder and brief prose:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a SQL server and database\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Test file to create:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/tests/Samples/Sample3_CreateServerAndDatabase.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Sql;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Sql.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Sql.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample3_CreateServerAndDatabase : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        public async Task CreateServerAndDatabaseAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Sql_CreateServerAndDatabase\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Create a SQL server\\n            SqlServerData serverData = new SqlServerData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                AdministratorLogin = \\u0026#34;myAdminUser\\u0026#34;,\\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = \\u0026#34;myAdminPassword123!\\u0026#34;,\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation serverOp = await resourceGroup.GetSqlServers()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myServer\\u0026#34;, serverData);\\n            SqlServerResource server = serverOp.Value;\\n\\n            // Create a database on the server\\n            SqlDatabaseData databaseData = new SqlDatabaseData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n            ArmOperation dbOp = await server.GetSqlDatabases()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myDatabase\\u0026#34;, databaseData);\\n            SqlDatabaseResource database = dbOp.Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created database: {database.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            // Cleanup\\n            await database.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            await server.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n- **Snippet name:** `Sql_CreateServerAndDatabase`\\n- **README location:** Inside the new `## Examples` section, under the `### Create a SQL server and database` heading\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` must contain these sections. \\u0026#34;myAdminPassword123!\\u0026#34;,\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation serverOp = await resourceGroup.GetSqlServers()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myServer\\u0026#34;, serverData);\\n            SqlServerResource server = serverOp.Value;\\n\\n            // Create a database on the server\\n            SqlDatabaseData databaseData = new SqlDatabaseData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n            ArmOperation dbOp = await server.GetSqlDatabases()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myDatabase\\u0026#34;, databaseData);\\n            SqlDatabaseResource database = dbOp.Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created database: {database.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            // Cleanup\\n            await database.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            await server.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n- **Snippet name:** `Sql_CreateServerAndDatabase`\\n- **README location:** Inside the new `## Examples` section, under the `### Create a SQL server and database` heading\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` must contain these sections. SqlDatabaseResource.SetTagsAsync() throws System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'id')\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen calling SetTagsAsync() from an SqlDatabaseResource object, the following error is thrown:\\r\\n\\r\\n\\u003e Microsoft.AspNetCore.Diagnostics.ExceptionHandlerMiddleware: An unhandled exception has occurred while executing the request.System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'id')at Azure.Core.Argument.AssertNotNull[T](T value, String name)at Azure.ResourceManager.ArmResource..ctor(ArmClient client, ResourceIdentifier id)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource..ctor(ArmClient client, ResourceIdentifier id)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource..ctor(ArmClient client, TagResourceData data)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResourceOperationSource.Azure.Core.IOperationSource\\u003cAzure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource\\u003e.CreateResultAsync(Response response, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.NextLinkOperationImplementation.OperationToOperationOfT`1.UpdateStateAsync(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationInternal`1.UpdateStatusAsync(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.UpdateStatusAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationPoller.WaitForCompletionAsync(Boolean async, OperationInternalBase operation, Nullable`1 delayHint, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\r\\n\\r\\nInterestingly, the tags are saved correctly. The error must occur after the save operation.\\r\\n\\r\\nImportant points:\\r\\n\\r\\n- The error only occurs when using a user managed identity (I host it in a Windows-based App Service).\\r\\n- When running locally using VisualStudioCredential(), no error occurs.\\r\\n- The error only occurs when Azure.ResourceManager v1.4 or later is used.\\r\\n- By default, Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 targets Azure.ResourceManager v1.3.2 - using these versions together results in no error.\\r\\n- Using Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 with Azure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 onwards causes the issue.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nI expect that I can use Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 with Azure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 onwards, and not receive an error when saving tags to a SqlDatabaseResource when accessed via a system managed identity.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nI receive the above error.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Create a .Net 6 MVC web application.\\r\\n2. Import the following NuGet package: Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0\\r\\n3. Import the following NuGet package: Microsoft.Identity.Web v2.16.1\\r\\n4. Add an AzureSql database (I have used DTU-based free tier).\\r\\n5. Create an App Service in Azure - Windows - .NET 6 runtime.\\r\\n6. Create a custom role which has \\\"Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/read\\\" and \\\"Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/write\\\" permissions.\\r\\n7. Create a system managed identity which applies the custom role on the database.\\r\\n8. Associate the system managed identity with the app service.\\r\\n\\r\\nAdd the following code into the home controller of the MVC project (fill in the todo parts with Azure info):\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\npublic async Task\\u003cIActionResult\\u003e Privacy()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    await SetTags();\\r\\n\\r\\n    return View();\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate async Task SetTags()\\r\\n{\\r\\
